source: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.37 + 1910.176 + NFPA 101 Life Safety Code
topic: Industrial egress, aisle widths, and safety clearances

facts.context: OSHA 1910.37(b) — exit routes must be at least 28 in wide
NFPA 101 — 44 in for corridors serving 50+ occupants
OSHA 1910.176(a) — aisles and passageways kept clear, min 48 in recommended
Forklift aisle — vehicle width (1.2m) + load + 0.6m each side clearance
Two-way forklift traffic — OSHA best practice (12 ft)
OSHA general industry — 36 in clearance for operator access around machines
OSHA / NEC 110.26 — 36 in minimum clear space in front of electrical panels
NEC 110.26 — 30 in minimum width of clear space at panels
NFPA 101 — 32 in clear opening minimum for emergency exit doors
OSHA 1910.157 — maintain 36 in clear access to fire extinguishers
NFPA 101 — dead-end corridors max 20 ft in industrial occupancy
ANSI/RMI MH16.1 — min aisle width for pallet racks (8 ft nominal)
Standard loading dock operational depth (12 ft)
OSHA 1910.179 — 24 in minimum clearance between crane and obstruction
